title: Cash-only outage household payment plan
summary: A small-denomination cash, receipt, priority, and fraud-control plan for periods when cards, cash machines, mobile networks, or banking applications are unavailable.
content:
A small-denomination cash, receipt, priority, and fraud-control plan for periods when cards, cash machines, mobile networks, or banking applications are unavailable.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: modest emergency cash in small denominations
steps: Define essential payment priorities such as water, food, medicines, transport, fuel, and safe lodging before an outage.
Store a modest amount securely in more than one protected location, record the total privately, and avoid displaying it.
During an outage use a written daily limit, obtain itemized receipts, note unpaid obligations, and verify prices before handing over cash.
After services return reconcile receipts and account activity, report duplicate or unauthorized charges through official channels, and replenish only what was used.
warnings: Do not keep all emergency money in one bag or reveal the amount publicly.
Treat urgent requests for codes, passwords, transfers, or gift cards as suspected fraud.
common_mistakes: Starting Cash-only outage household payment plan without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Cash-only outage household payment plan.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Cash-only outage household payment plan when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Cash-only outage household payment plan when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Cash-only outage household payment plan.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Cash-only outage household payment plan safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Cash-only outage household payment plan when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Cash-only outage household payment plan.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Cash-only outage household payment plan should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Cash-only outage household payment plan whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Cash-only outage household payment plan in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Cash-only outage household payment plan after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Cash-only outage household payment plan cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Cash-only outage household payment plan are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Cash-only outage household payment plan to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Cash-only outage household payment plan.']
